titleStmt.title: Letter from Mrs E.N. Gilbert to Nancy O'Rahilly, 21 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E.N. Gilbert

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter of condolence from Mrs. E.N. Gilbert to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). The writer shares Mrs. O'Rahilly's grief at the death of her husband and consoles her that she has her sons to live for and the 'glorious memory'. She was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later.

correspAction.placeName: 823 N. 20th Street, Philadelphia, United States of America.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
May 21, 1916 825 N. 20th St Philadelphia Dear, dear Nancie, My heart is break
ing for you. I must send you a
word. I am thinking of my dear little
chum in the happy days as Long
Branch and now what bitter
bitter matters have engulfed you.
Not engulfed you tho' thank God
you have your sons to live for
and a glorious memory to make
patience and fortitude and great
achievement possible for you all,
I can do nothing for you dear
but live and grieve with you and
for you and pray for you always. With tenderest love, yours always E.N.G I just heard from Madeleine yesterday.
